title: Overview
summary: High-level overview of the AI-powered documentation generation system

blocks.text: The documentation generation system is an **AI-powered tool** that automatically creates comprehensive documentation for codebases. It uses a multi-stage pipeline with specialized agents to plan, write, and review documentation pages based on code analysis.
The primary purpose of this system is to **automate the documentation process** by:

items.text: Analyzing codebases to understand structure and functionality
Generating structured documentation plans with navigation
Writing detailed documentation pages using AI agents
Reviewing and improving content quality through iterative refinement

blocks.text: The system follows an **event-driven architecture** with three main components:

items.text: **CLI Interface** - Entry point for generating documentation
**Worker Pipeline** - Event-driven processing of documentation generation
**Agent System** - Specialized AI agents for planning, writing, and reviewing

blocks.text: The command-line interface provides the main entry point for documentation generation:
The event-driven worker processes documentation generation through a multi-stage pipeline:

items.text: **Indexing** - Create vector embeddings for semantic search
**Planning** - Generate documentation structure and page specifications

blocks.text: Three specialized AI agents work together to create high-quality documentation:

rows: Creates documentation plan with navigation structure and page specifications
Writes individual documentation pages based on code analysis
Reviews pages for accuracy, quality, and identifies hallucinations

items.text: **Semantic Search** - Uses vector embeddings to find relevant code sections
**Iterative Refinement** - Multiple review cycles to improve quality
**Hallucination Detection** - Identifies and corrects AI-generated inaccuracies
**Configurable Quality Thresholds** - Customizable scoring and retry limits

blocks.text: The system is designed to handle codebases of various sizes and produce documentation that accurately reflects the code structure and functionality.
💡 **Tip**: Use the `--clear-qdrant` flag to reset the vector database when re-generating documentation for the same codebase.
